mnemonics: MVCC: My Version Creates Consistency (by keeping multiple versions of data items to allow concurrent readers and writers without blocking).
keyPoints: MVCC allows multiple transactions to access the database simultaneously by maintaining different versions of a data item.
Read operations access a snapshot of the database from a past timestamp, ensuring a consistent view without locks.
Write operations create a new version of the data item, leaving older versions available for ongoing reads.
commonMistakes: Confusing MVCC with locking protocols; MVCC avoids reader-writer conflicts through versioning, not locks.
Assuming that a read will always see the very latest committed write; it sees the latest commit as of its start time.
Forgetting that old versions must be garbage collected to reclaim storage, which is a key administrative task.
